input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete plan for setting up .NET MAUI on Windows 11 targeting Android + Windows:

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK

```powershell
# Install latest .NET SDK (includes MAUI workload support)
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9

# Verify
dotnet --version
```

## 2. Install the MAUI Workload

```powershell
# List available workloads to discover current MAUI workload name
dotnet workload search maui

# Install it
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install Android SDK (via the .NET Android workload)

The `maui` workload pulls in `android` automatically. To confirm and manage Android components:

```powershell
# Check what was installed
dotnet workload list

# The Android SDK gets placed here by default:
# $env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk
# Set ANDROID_HOME if not already set:
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME",
  "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk", "User")
```

## 4. Accept Android Licenses

```powershell
& "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses
```

## 5. Discover & Install Specific Android API Levels

```powershell
# List available platforms dynamically
& "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list |
  Select-String "platforms;android-"

# Install a specific API (e.g., 34)
& "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" "platforms;android-34" "build-tools;34.0.0"
```

## 6. Install Windows App SDK (for WinUI 3 / Windows target)

```powershell
# Discover latest version
winget search "Windows App SDK"

# Install the runtime
winget install Microsoft.WindowsAppSDK.1.6

# Or install via VS Build Tools workload (see step 7)
```

## 7. Install Visual Studio Build Tools (if not using full VS)

```powershell
# Discover available workload component IDs
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.BuildTools

# Then add required components
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" modify `
  --installPath "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\BuildTools" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.ManagedDesktop `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.Maui.All `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.WindowsAppSDK.Cs `
  --quiet --wait
```

## 8. Enable Developer Mode (required for Windows target)

```powershell
# Enable Developer Mode via registry
reg add "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
  /t REG_DWORD /f /v AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense /d 1
```

## 9. Create & Build a Test Project

```powershell
# List MAUI templates to discover exact template names
dotnet new list maui

# Create project
dotnet new maui -n MyMauiApp
cd MyMauiApp

# Build for Windows
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android
```

## 10. Run

```powershell
# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Run on Android emulator (ensure emulator is running)
dotnet run -f net9.0-android
```

---

**Key "discover dynamically" commands summary:**

| What | Command |
|------|---------|
| Available workloads | `dotnet workload search` |
| Installed workloads | `dotnet workload list` |
| Android APIs | `sdkmanager.bat --list` |
| .NET SDKs available | `winget search Microsoft.DotNet.SDK` |
| MAUI templates | `dotnet new list maui` |
| Target frameworks | Check `<TargetFrameworks>` in `.csproj` |

Adjust `net9.0` to whatever `dotnet --version` reports as your major version.
